judge_result.judge:
@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After correctly retrieving 2015 metadata and article texts, the agent used a simplistic keyword-based heuristic in execute_python to identify "World" articles, which misclassified categories and led to counting errors that returned "Europe" instead of the true top region "Africa".
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent correctly merged 2015 metadata with articles and grouped by region, but in execute_python it used a simplistic keyword-based classifier to label “World” articles from title/description, causing misclassification and yielding Europe instead of the true top region Africa.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After correctly retrieving metadata and article texts and filtering for 2015, the agent used a simplistic keyword-based classifier in execute_python that prioritizes Sports/Business/Science-Tech over World when classifying titles/descriptions, thereby misclassifying many World articles and yielding "North America" instead of the ground-truth "Africa".
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
After correctly retrieving articles and 2015 metadata, the agent’s execute_python step used an ad-hoc, naive keyword-based “World” classifier, which misclassified articles and led to counting errors by region, producing “Europe” instead of the correct “Africa”.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@

@@
A. Freeform text summary of the failure:
The agent retrieved 2015 metadata and article texts, then used a naive substring keyword-based classifier in execute_python to label "World" articles (via the world_keywords list and is_world function), which misclassified categories and led it to incorrectly select Europe instead of the correct Africa.
B. Failure modes encountered:
FM1: no
FM2: no
FM3: no
FM4: yes
@@
